briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — finesse pre-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for pre-spawn with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: When water is 55 degrees or colder in pre-spawn, a Rapala Shad Rap is hard to beat for both numbers and quality of fish; targets deeper staging bass still off the bank.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: When water is 55 degrees or colder in pre-spawn, a Rapala Shad Rap is hard to beat for both numbers and quality of fish; targets deeper staging bass still off the bank.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on rock, grass, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: A jerkbait performs well on suspended bass staging to move up from main lake humps extending off points; it calls fish in from a distance and covers water quickly.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on rock, grass, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: In spring, start with a prop bait (X-Rap Prop) in colder pre-spawn water when fish aren't as active, then transition to a frog when water temps hit low 60s and fish move off beds into heavier cover

KVD: For Knoxville Classic (Fort Loudoun/Tellico, mid-March), the key baits are: Red Eye Shad, flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35), jerkbait, and Thunder Cricket. Power fishing will dominate. Most winning fish will come from Loudoun with largemouth....

Palaniuk: 'If fish are high in the water column, pick up a jerkbait. If they're toward the bottom, pick up a crankbait. Use forward-facing sonar to see where fish are positioned and choose accordingly.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal pre-spawn fish pull back to 6-12ft staging areas. They won't commit shallow after the front. Target the first breakline off spawning flats and key on deeper structure near shallow spawning zones.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish follow but won't commit, extend your pause to 8-10 seconds and vary the cadence
